does: This paper addresses circuit discovery in mechanistic interpretability by proposing three technical improvements to edge selection methods. The authors employ bootstrapping to identify edges with statistically consistent attribution scores, introduce a ratio-based selection strategy that prioritizes strong positive-scoring edges while balancing model performance against circuit faithfulness, and replace greedy selection with an integer linear programming formulation for more principled optimization. These methods are evaluated on the Mechanistic Interpretability Benchmark across multiple tasks and models, demonstrating improved faithfulness metrics and performance compared to baseline approaches.
connects: This work builds directly on the Mechanistic Interpretability Benchmark framework and extends prior circuit discovery methods that typically rely on greedy attribution-based selection. The paper responds to fundamental challenges in identifying faithful circuits—subgraphs of neural networks that causally implement specific computations. It connects to existing literature on neural network interpretability, attribution methods, and circuit analysis that seek to understand learned representations. The use of integer linear programming for circuit selection draws on optimization techniques in machine learning, while the bootstrapping approach incorporates statistical robustness methods from causal inference and empirical validation traditions in interpretability research.
enables: This work advances the technical toolkit for mechanistic interpretability research, enabling more reliable circuit discovery that could support downstream applications in model auditing, safety analysis, and understanding failure modes. The improved faithfulness metrics may help researchers more confidently attribute model behavior to specific circuit components, facilitating transfer of findings across model architectures and scales. The integer linear programming formulation and bootstrapping methodology provide generalizable techniques applicable to circuit discovery in other architectures and tasks. These advances could accelerate research into mechanistic understanding of language models and support efforts to identify circuits responsible for reasoning, knowledge recall, and potentially deceptive behaviors in larger models.
